headline: Anthropic's release of Claude Fable 5 (Mythos) dominates this cycle, with educators dissecting its benchmarks, agent features, and deployment patterns.
body: Anthropic shipped Claude Mythos, its first model in the Mythos class, posting top results on SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code, with vision, long-running tasks, and extended memory [11]. Cyril Imhof frames Fable 5.0 as a top-tier category distinct from Opus that strategically deploys Haiku, Sonnet, and Opus, and highlights a Know Your Agent (KYA) capability for regulated deployments [1][12][13]. The comparison coverage is uneven: one video puts Opus 4.8 ahead on agentic coding and knowledge work while Fable shows limits [8], another claims Fable 5.0 and GPT 5.5 break benchmark records including Exploit Bench [9][10]. Better Stack separately covers PAI, a Claude Code Life OS with persistent memory, custom skills, and a seven-phase observe-to-learn workflow [0], and 3Blue1Brown examines how to evaluate whether an encoding is optimal [2].
